summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Anonymat & Pseudonymat feature. All 24 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI tests verified auth modal, registration, login, privacy settings, and account upgrade flows.
test_credentials: Test accounts created dynamically with unique pseudos during testing
seed_data_creation: No seed data needed - tests create their own test accounts
context_for_next_testing_agent: Anonymat/Pseudonymat feature fully tested. Backend endpoints: /api/auth/register, /api/auth/login, /api/auth/verify, /api/auth/check-pseudo, /api/auth/upgrade, /api/auth/change-password, /api/profile/privacy, /api/auth/export-data, /api/auth/account (DELETE), /api/auth/anonymous. Frontend: AuthModal with login/register tabs, PrivacySettingsView at /dashboard/confidentialite. All data-testid attributes properly implemented.

backend_apis: GET /api/auth/verify - Token verification with auth_mode, pseudo, identity_level
POST /api/auth/change-password - Change password for pseudo accounts
PUT /api/profile/privacy - Update visibility, display_name, bio, consent_marketing
GET /api/auth/export-data - Export all user data as JSON
DELETE /api/auth/account - Delete account and all data
GET /api/profile - Verified password_hash is NOT returned
frontend_flows: Auth modal opens from Landing page with login/register tabs
Registration form with privacy reassurance, pseudo, password, confirm, optional email, CGU+Privacy checkboxes
Dashboard shows auth mode badge (Anonyme vs pseudo name)
Privacy settings: account status, visibility level selector, display name/bio, consents, export/delete

security: password_hash never returned in API responses - verified
Change password requires current password - verified
Anonymous accounts cannot change password - verified
